title: SwiftUI Basics
description: Learn the fundamentals of SwiftUI - views, modifiers, and the declarative UI paradigm.

sections.content:
## Introduction to SwiftUI

SwiftUI is Apple's modern framework for building user interfaces across all Apple platforms.

### Declarative vs Imperative

**Imperative** (UIKit): "Do this, then do that"
```swift
let label = UILabel()
label.text = "Hello"
view.addSubview(label)
```

**Declarative** (SwiftUI): "This is what I want"
```swift
Text("Hello")
```

### Views

Everything you see is a **View**:
- `Text("Hello")` - Display text
- `Image(systemName: "star")` - Display an image
- `Button("Click me") { }` - A tappable button
- `VStack { }` - Vertical stack of views

sections.title: Your First SwiftUI View
sections.expectedOutput: A centered card with a star, title, and subtitle

sections.content:
### Modifiers

Modifiers change a view's appearance or behavior:

```swift
Text("Hello")
    .font(.title)           // Change font
    .foregroundColor(.blue) // Change color
    .padding()              // Add space
    .background(Color.yellow) // Add background
```

### Composing Views

Combine simple views to build complex interfaces:

```swift
VStack {           // Vertical stack
    HStack {       // Horizontal stack inside
        Text("Left")
        Text("Right")
    }
}
```

sections.title: Try It: Build a Profile Card
sections.instructions: Create a VStack with an image, name, and description using what you learned.

sections.question: What does the .padding() modifier do?
sections.explanation: .padding() adds space around a view, creating visual breathing room.
